summary: CRITICAL — Requires General Counsel review before any further engagement. Defense contractor NDA with ITAR (International Traffic in Arms Regulations) compliance obligations embedded in section 6 that are not standard NDA language and carry criminal liability. Perpetual obligations with no sunset. Our company as receiving party accepts personal liability for any employees who breach — clause 7.2 attempts to make officers personally liable. Liquidated damages of USD 2M per breach plus 'all costs of enforcement including attorney fees.' Governing law is Virginia with mandatory arbitration under AAA Commercial Rules — expensive but manageable. Two clauses missing. The ITAR provisions alone require specialist export control legal review before signing.

playbook_results.deviation: Definition incorporates ITAR-controlled technical data by reference to 22 CFR Part 120-130. This means any breach of this NDA may simultaneously constitute an ITAR violation carrying criminal penalties of up to USD 1M per violation and 10 years imprisonment. Standard NDA playbook does not contemplate ITAR obligations — specialist review required.

Section 7.2 attempts to impose personal liability on 'officers, directors, and key employees of Receiving Party.' Playbook requires obligations to rest with the legal entity only. Officer personal liability is non-standard and likely unenforceable but will generate significant litigation risk.

Perpetual obligation for any information classified as ITAR-controlled technical data. Non-ITAR information has a 10-year term. Dual-tier structure creates complexity and the perpetual ITAR tier cannot be negotiated away under US export control law.

Compelled disclosure carve-out is heavily restricted: any disclosure required by law must first obtain consent from Stratos Defense — which may be impossible in practice. ITAR does not permit blanket pre-consent requirements to override regulatory obligations.

Destruction requires DOD-compliant data destruction methods per NIST SP 800-88. Standard office equipment may not comply. Certification must be signed by a 'security-cleared officer' — we may not have cleared personnel.

Liquidated damages of USD 2,000,000 per breach, plus all enforcement costs and attorney fees. Pre-consented injunctive relief without bond. The USD 2M per-breach figure is punitive rather than compensatory and likely unconscionable — but defending that position is expensive.

Virginia law with mandatory AAA Commercial Arbitration. Three-arbitrator panel at USD 15,000–25,000 per arbitrator per day. Expensive but enforceable. Arbitration confidentiality clause prevents us from disclosing the dispute — including to regulators.
One-way NDA accurately represents arrangement. We are receiving party. Correctly documented.

ITAR-controlled information is explicitly carved out from all four standard exclusions. Information that enters the public domain, was previously known, or is independently developed — if it falls within the ITAR definition — remains confidential. This is legally inconsistent and creates perpetual obligations on information we may have developed ourselves.

Absent. In the context of defense technical data, the absence of a no-license clause creates significant ambiguity about what rights we have to use information shared with us for evaluation purposes. Particularly dangerous given ITAR overlay.

critical_flags: CL-001: ITAR criminal liability embedded — requires export control specialist review
CL-002: Officer personal liability clause — non-standard, reject
CL-003: Perpetual obligation for ITAR-classified information — cannot be negotiated
CL-006: USD 2M per-breach liquidated damages — punitive, challenge enforceability
CL-009: Standard exclusions eliminated for ITAR information — impossible obligation
recommended_redlines: CL-001: Engage export control counsel before responding. Do not sign pending specialist review.
CL-002: Delete section 7.2 officer personal liability entirely. Entity-level obligation only.
CL-006: Replace USD 2M liquidated damages with 'actual proven damages.' Remove attorney fee shifting.
CL-009: Restore independent development exclusion for information we develop independently, regardless of ITAR status.
CL-010: Insert explicit no-license clause covering both NDA and ITAR-controlled information.
